description_long: This is a medium-sized, irregularly shaped stone with a rugged and uneven surface, featuring a mix of blue-gray and off-white coloration. The stone has a prominent ridge running along one side, with one face appearing smoother and lighter, while the opposite side is darker and more textured. Subtle greenish hues are visible in some areas, suggesting possible moss or lichen growth. The surface is coarse and pitted, with no visible artificial markings or carvings, indicating natural weathering. Its mass and density suggest it is composed primarily of solid rock, likely granite or basalt. The stone would serve as a natural obstacle or decorative element in an urban park, sidewalk, or landscaped area.
description: A rugged, blue-gray stone with an irregular shape, coarse texture, and subtle color variations. It features a prominent ridge and areas of both smooth and rough surfaces.
description_view_0: The stone is seen from above, highlighting its blue-gray ridge and the contrast between the lighter and darker sides.
description_view_1: This view shows the broader, lighter face with greenish patches and a more rounded profile.
description_view_2: The stone appears narrower, with the ridge and a mix of dark and light textures visible along the edge.
description_view_3: The darkest, roughest side is visible, with minimal color variation and a more angular appearance.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or natural barrier on the ground
attribute_pattern: Irregular mottling and subtle color banding, with no artificial carvings or regular patterns.
attribute_weathering: Rough and pitted, with some areas showing signs of lichen or moss, indicating moderate natural weathering.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Blends naturally with soil, grass, or other stones, and may be partially surrounded by vegetation or urban landscaping elements.
